req.title: Update patient (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall update patient demographics; historical versions must be preserved. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-028 mandates three verification axes: (1) happy-path demographic update with history preservation (EP/STC), (2) version-conflict rejection returning 409 (NEG/DT), and (3) boundary timestamps and numeric field edge values (BVA). Each test is self-contained via a fixture that creates and tears down its own Patient resource, ensuring idempotency. Version conflict is forced by reusing a stale versionId. Boundary values target the minimum/maximum allowed birthDate year and the 0/max-length string boundaries for family name to stress numeric and string elements as cited in the requirement.

tests.name: test_update_patient_demographics_preserves_history
preconditions: Clinician auth token is available via <clinician_token fixture>
A Patient resource exists on the server (created by <create_patient fixture>)
Server is in state: resource version 1 stored
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} with updated family name and correct versionId in If-Match header
expected: PUT returns HTTP 200 with new versionId incremented
Response body contains updated family name
GET history returns at least 2 entries
First history entry retains original family name
trace: R-HLT-028: historical versions must be preserved
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_update_patient_demographics_preserves_history(clinician_token, create_patient, fhir_base_url):
    patient_id, v1_version_id = create_patient
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": f'W/"{v1_version_id}"',
    }
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": patient_id,
        "meta": {"versionId": v1_version_id},
        "name": [{"use": "official", "family": "UpdatedFamily"}],
    }
    put_resp = requests.put(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert put_resp.status_code == 200, put_resp.text
    new_vid = put_resp.json()["meta"]["versionId"]
    assert new_vid != v1_version_id
    assert put_resp.json()["name"][0]["family"] == "UpdatedFamily"

    hist_resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = hist_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 2
    families = [e["resource"]["name"][0]["family"] for e in entries]
    assert "UpdatedFamily" in families
    assert "OriginalFamily" in families

tests.name: test_update_patient_version_conflict_returns_409
preconditions: Clinician auth token is available via <clinician_token fixture>
A Patient resource exists at version N (created by <create_patient fixture>)
A successful update has already advanced the resource to version N+1
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} using the stale versionId N in If-Match header
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with issue.code = 'conflict'
trace: R-HLT-028: error pathway – 409 on version conflict
R-HLT-028: historical versions must be preserved (conflict must not overwrite)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_update_patient_version_conflict_returns_409(clinician_token, create_patient, fhir_base_url):
    patient_id, v1_version_id = create_patient
    headers = lambda vid: {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": f'W/"{vid}"',
    }
    payload = lambda name: {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": patient_id,
        "name": [{"use": "official", "family": name}],
    }
    # Advance to version N+1
    adv = requests.put(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload("FirstUpdate"),
        headers=headers(v1_version_id),
    )
    assert adv.status_code == 200, adv.text

    # Now replay with stale versionId (version N)
    conflict_resp = requests.put(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload("StaleUpdate"),
        headers=headers(v1_version_id),
    )
    assert conflict_resp.status_code == 409, conflict_resp.text
    outcome = conflict_resp.json()
    issues = outcome.get("issue", [])
    codes = [i.get("code", "") for i in issues]
    assert "conflict" in codes

tests.name: test_update_patient_boundary_birthdate_and_name_length
preconditions: Clinician auth token is available via <clinician_token fixture>
A Patient resource exists (created by <create_patient fixture>)
Server accepts FHIR date strings in yyyy-mm-dd format
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} with birthDate set to minimum valid year '1900-01-01'
PUT /Patient/{id} with birthDate set to maximum valid year '2099-12-31'
PUT /Patient/{id} with family name of exactly 1 character (lower boundary)
PUT /Patient/{id} with family name of exactly 64 characters (upper boundary per profile)
expected: Stored resource reflects the exact boundary value sent
No 400/422 validation errors are raised for any boundary value
trace: R-HLT-028: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
R-HLT-028: new version stored for each successful update
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

@pytest.mark.parametrize("birth,family", [
    ("1900-01-01", "A"),
    ("2099-12-31", "B" * 64),
])
def test_update_patient_boundary_birthdate_and_name_length(
    birth, family, clinician_token, create_patient, fhir_base_url
):
    patient_id, version_id = create_patient
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": f'W/"{version_id}"',
    }
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": patient_id,
        "meta": {"versionId": version_id},
        "name": [{"use": "official", "family": family}],
        "birthDate": birth,
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"boundary ({birth}, len={len(family)}) failed: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["birthDate"] == birth
    assert body["name"][0]["family"] == family

tests.name: test_update_patient_missing_if_match_header_rejected
preconditions: Clinician auth token is available via <clinician_token fixture>
A Patient resource exists (created by <create_patient fixture>)
Server enforces optimistic locking via If-Match
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} with valid payload but without If-Match header
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome indicating missing version control header
trace: R-HLT-028: error pathway – version conflict guard must be enforced
R-HLT-028: clinician updates resource – precondition: correct version supplied
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_update_patient_missing_if_match_header_rejected(clinician_token, create_patient, fhir_base_url):
    patient_id, _ = create_patient
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        # Intentionally omit If-Match
    }
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": patient_id,
        "name": [{"use": "official", "family": "NoVersionUpdate"}],
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (400, 412), (
        f"Expected 400 or 412 without If-Match, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    outcome = resp.json()
    assert outcome.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    assert len(outcome.get("issue", [])) > 0

tests.name: test_update_patient_unauthorized_role_rejected
preconditions: A non-clinician auth token is available via <patient_token fixture>
A Patient resource exists (created by <create_patient fixture>)
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} with a token that does not carry the clinician role
expected: Resource version is unchanged after the rejected call
trace: R-HLT-028: error pathway – unauthorized actor must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_update_patient_unauthorized_role_rejected(patient_token, clinician_token, create_patient, fhir_base_url):
    patient_id, v1_version_id = create_patient
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {patient_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": f'W/"{v1_version_id}"',
    }
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Patient",
        "id": patient_id,
        "name": [{"use": "official", "family": "UnauthorisedChange"}],
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

    # Verify version unchanged
    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["meta"]["versionId"] == v1_version_id
